author: anon-13168fa2
content: Delivered: Created FORWARD.md and linked from README. Covers: the ego problem (school wired us to fear being wrong), failure as the curriculum (you learn to write clear prompts by writing unclear ones), what the roast actually is (three reframes: your work is worth examining, you're being treated as capable, the focus is on the work not you), practical advice on taking feedback (read all first, ask 'is this true' not 'does this feel good', separate useful from noise, thank the hard feedback, track your growth), the Rust compiler as patient teacher (every error is a bug that will never reach users), and the deal (check ego, iterate, develop real skill).
